ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-20-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF CARL SHERMER'S MOTION FOR ORDER SEVERING CASE FROM GROUP AND PROMPTLY ASSIGNING OUT FOR TRIAL IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE CASE IS SEVERED FROM BRAYTON GROUP 436. THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 3/17/08 @ 11:15 AM IN DEPT. 206. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCES SET FOR 3/6/08, 3/10/08, 3/12/08, 3/13/08, AND 3/14/08 @ 10:30 AM IN DEPT. 220. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND IS DUE BY 3/3/08. DEFENDANTS RESPONSE IS DUE BY 3/5/08. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 12/20/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 1/10/08. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: EBURA; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE CSR #4280 (206)

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Meng-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey,CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Tim Johnson, as Successor-in-Interest to Carl Shermer, Deceased, and Christina Mourched, as Wrongful Death Heir of Carl Shermer, Deceased.
Mr. Johnson and Ms. Mourched were placed under oath and testified.
Kristine Elam, a witness for Tim Johnson, was placed under oath but did not testify.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds from settlements negotiated after the January 15, 2013 death of Carl Shermer in the following proportions as follows:

Estate of James Riggins
(Payable to Tim Johnson) - 80%.
Christina Mourched
as Wrongful Death Heir - 20%.

All future net funds derived from settlements entered after the January 15, 2013 death of Carl Shermer shall be distributed in the percentages set forth in paragraph 1 herein above.

The signature of plaintiff Tim Johnson alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.

Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SQUARE D COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
APPEARANCES: LAUREL HALBANY, ESQUIRE (BRAYTON PURCELL, 415.898.1555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. BRENDAN J. TUOHY, ESQUIRE (K&L GATES, 415.249.1077) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS LATE ISSUED TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUED THE MATTER TO MARCH 13, 2008 TO ALLOW FOR THE TAKING OF A DEPOSITION. LATE ISSUED TENTATIVE RULING (NOT APPLIED) IS AS FOLLOWS: Square D's motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is granted. Defendant has shifted the burden of proof. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are overruled. Plaintiff's evidence does not create a triable issue of fact in that there is an inability to link the products Mr. Shermer worked on with Square D products that contain asbestos. Plaintiff has not established good cause to continue this motion. Plaintiff has not explained the delay in seeking the evidence and has not explained how the testimony sought will establish the missing link in its proof. Given that plaintiff's counsel has deposed Square D parties in other cases, it is reasonable to require that plaintiff provide a specific description of what the expected testimony will be. (302/PJM/VC) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
